result:
database connection pool = pre-made pool of open DB connections, reused across requests
opening TCP+TLS+auth handshake per query = slow, so keep N warm connections sitting idle
app borrows one, runs query, returns it — no handshake cost
pool has max size (cap on concurrent DB load) and min/idle size (keep ready)
if all busy, new caller waits in queue or gets timeout
idle connections pinged/recycled so DB doesn't kill them for inactivity
tune max to DB's connection limit ÷ number of app instances, else you starve other services
too small = request queue backs up, too large = DB chokes on context switches
serverless messes with this — each lambda = own pool, fanout kills DB, use pgbouncer or proxy
pooler modes: session (sticky, safe), transaction (swap between queries, faster, breaks prepared statements + session state), statement (rare)
